{{Quote|Coruscant's space construction platforms are remarkable examples of Imperial industriousness. Here giant-size habitation spheres are designed and assembled strictly for peacetime purposes.|[[Pollux Hax/Legends|Pollux Hax]]|<ref name="The Illustrated Star Wars Universe" />}}
The '''Death Star III''', also known as the '''third Death Star''', was a mock [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] [[battle station]] created from an incomplete [[worldcraft]] by the [[Restored Empire|Kaarenth Dissension]].<ref name="Warlord" /> In [[4 ABY/Legends|4.3 ABY]],<ref name="KOTHB" /> [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Ennix Devian]] of the Kaarenth Dissension used this false Death Star to create a [[Battle of Death Star III|diversion]] at the temporary [[Alliance of Free Planets]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] while his forces stealthily hijacked countless [[Warship/Legends|warships]] from neighboring Alliance [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]].<ref name="Warlord" /> While the Kaarenth Dissension's sophisticated feint was a successful distraction, this so-called Death Star III was ultimately destroyed by [[Red Squadron (Star Tours)|Red Squadron]] when [[Red Leader (Star Tours)|Red Leader]] made a direct hit to its unprotected [[Thermal exhaust port/Legends|thermal exhaust port]].<ref name="Star Tours" />

==Characteristics==
[[File:Habitation_spheres.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Death Star III worldcraft prior to its modification.]]
The exact measurements and capabilities of the station are not completely known, except that it was armed with a [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] similar to the other stations in the series and had a relatively heavier defense network of [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] dotting the surface.<ref name="Star Tours" />

The station was never completed and at the time of its destruction, scaffolding was still present in the trench leading to its weak spot, creating obstacles for would-be attackers. Nonetheless, the station had been nearing finalization, evidenced by its looking more whole compared to the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]].<ref name="Star Tours" />

Despite it being created either during or after the Death Star II, which had removed various design flaws that [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|doomed]] the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first]] Death Star, it still shared the same design flaw as the first Death Star: a [[Thermal exhaust port/Legends|thermal exhaust port]] located within one of the trenches.<ref name="Star Tours" /><ref name="Convenient Daily Departures: The History of Star Tours">{{SW|url=news/convenient-daily-departures-the-history-of-star-tours|text=Convenient Daily Departures: The History of ''Star Tours''|int=Convenient Daily Departures: The History of Star Tours|nolive=1}}</ref>

==History==
[[File:StarTours-Death_Star_III.jpg|180px|thumb|Death Star III behind [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], on a StarSpeeder 3000 video screen]]
{{Quote|I've always wanted to do this! We're going in!|[[RX-24/Legends|RX-24]] begins his trench run on the Death Star III|<ref name="Star Tours" />}}
During the reign of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] manufactured a select number of [[worldcraft]]s,<ref name="The Crystal Star">''[[The Crystal Star]]''</ref> a large type of [[habitation sphere]]s<ref name="Warlord" /> that resembled natural [[Planet/Legends|planetoids]] and could travel through both [[Realspace/Legends|realspace]] and [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].<ref name="The Crystal Star" /> Those exceptional [[Starship/Legends|starships]] were all commissioned by the Emperor himself as a gift to his most devoted servants.<ref name="TEGTC">''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> Two of those worldcrafts, which had been intended to be supplied to [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Ardus Kaine]] and the Emperor's personal hitman [[Ennix Devian]], were being built in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world of the Empire.<ref name="Warlord" />

Shortly after Palpatine's death at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Devian stole both unfinished worldcrafts. After sending them to two separate corners of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], he reconverted one of them into a [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]-like battle station with the intention of creating a feint against the enemy [[Alliance of Free Planets]], who at that [[Time/Legends|time]] were based at Endor.<ref name="Warlord" />

[[File:Startours_DSIII.jpg|thumb|left|180px|The Star Tours speeder attacked on Death Star III]]
The battle station underwent construction near [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] and was guarded by a fleet of [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]].<ref name="Star Tours" /> Its exact date of it commencing construction, however, remained a mystery,<ref name="Convenient Daily Departures: The History of Star Tours" /> although it was closer to completion a few months after Endor.

A few months after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] task force was sent to destroy the station.<ref name="KOTHB" /> A similar weakness to the first Death Star had been discovered in the unfinished superstructure, and Republic [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] made their way past the Imperial escorts, fought [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE]] squadrons and made a trench run to their designated target, as well as having to defend an inexperienced [[StarSpeeder 3000]] [[Star Tours/Legends|Star Tours]] [[RX-24/Legends|pilot]] that accidentally came into the conflict.<ref name="Star Tours" />

Firing [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] into the [[Thermal exhaust port/Legends|thermal exhaust port]],<ref name="Convenient Daily Departures: The History of Star Tours" /> the fighters withdrew from the battle, leaving the station to be destroyed.<ref name="Star Tours" /> Ultimately, however, its destruction acted as a Pyrrhic victory for the Rebels, as Devian used the distraction caused by the battle to steal several vessels from various shipyards owned by the New Republic.

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|The Star Wars are now over, for the most part, although rumors persist about another Death Star orbiting far out in the galaxies.|''Star Wars'': The Disneyland Journey, Part One|<ref name="Starlog 118">{{Starlog|118|Star Wars: The Disneyland Journey, Part One|''Star Wars'': The Disneyland Journey, Part One}}</ref>}}
Early press information for [[The Walt Disney Company|Disney's]] [[Star Tours (real-world)|''Star Tours'']] ride placed the attraction's setting after {{Film|VI}}, and first mentioned that the Death Star seen in it was a new one.<ref name="Starlog 118" /> Many years later, [[Lucasfilm]]'s [[Leland Chee]] first used the term "Death Star III" and placed its destruction a few months after Endor in a blog post.<ref name="KOTHB" /> This was later confirmed as canon in the article [[Convenient Daily Departures: The History of Star Tours]] on StarWars.com.<ref name="Convenient Daily Departures: The History of Star Tours" />

The station being built near the Forest Moon of Endor like the Death Star II before it is similar to an original concept for [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']], where two Death Stars would have been built near [[Had Abbadon]] (then the [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial capital world]]). Later sources then used the concept art as the basis for [[habitation sphere]]s. Based on the Imperials' suspicious claims that they were designed strictly for peaceful purposes, some fans suggested them as the origin of the new Death Star. This was later revealed to be the case in Part 2 of the StarWars.com Blog series [[The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire]].<ref name="Warlord" />

In the non-canon game [[Star Wars: Tiny Death Star|''Tiny Death Star'']], a random [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] entry states that one of the residents of the Death Star is simply staying there until he can afford to stay at the Death Star 3.<ref name="Tiny">''[[Star Wars: Tiny Death Star]]''</ref>
